channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Youth Impact Bootcamp LATAM 2026: Fully Funded International Startup Accelerator for Young Social Entrepreneurs in Latin America
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Youth Impact Bootcamp LATAM 2026, an exciting international entrepreneurship programme designed to empower young impact entrepreneurs across Latin America. Implemented under the EU-LAC Social Accelerator Project, the bootcamp provides practical training, international exposure, peer networking, expert coaching, and investment readiness support for startups addressing social and environmental challenges. The […]

The post Youth Impact Bootcamp LATAM 2026: Fully Funded International Startup Accelerator for Young Social Entrepreneurs in Latin America appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Youth Impact Bootcamp LATAM 2026, an exciting international entrepreneurship programme designed to empower young impact entrepreneurs across Latin America. Implemented under the EU-LAC Social Accelerator Project, the bootcamp provides practical training, international exposure, peer networking, expert coaching, and investment readiness support for startups addressing social and environmental challenges.

The programme is implemented by the International Trade Centre (ITC) in partnership with a consortium comprising:

  • CAINCO
  • Caribbean Export Development Agency
  • Central American Bank for Economic Integration (CACB)
  • Tecnalia
  • Tecnológico de Monterrey

The programme supports young entrepreneurs developing innovative businesses that contribute to the green, digital, and inclusive transition in Latin America, in alignment with the European Union’s Global Gateway Agenda.

Delivered entirely in Spanish, the Youth Impact Bootcamp LATAM offers founders an opportunity to strengthen their businesses, develop practical entrepreneurial skills, expand professional networks, and prepare for international growth opportunities.

About the Youth Impact Bootcamp LATAM

The Youth Impact Bootcamp LATAM is a comprehensive accelerator programme created specifically for entrepreneurs who are already building businesses and need practical tools rather than classroom-based theory.

Unlike traditional entrepreneurship courses, the programme combines:

  • Live expert-led masterclasses
  • Self-paced digital learning
  • Practical business challenges
  • Peer-to-peer learning
  • Networking opportunities
  • Personalized coaching
  • International exposure

The bootcamp is community-driven and designed to produce measurable progress in participants’ businesses throughout the programme.

Programme Objectives

The programme seeks to:

  • Strengthen impact-driven startups across Latin America
  • Support businesses contributing to green, digital, and inclusive economies
  • Prepare entrepreneurs for investment and international expansion
  • Build stronger entrepreneurial ecosystems
  • Promote innovation aligned with the European Union Global Gateway priorities
  • Connect founders with international partners and investors

Programme Structure

The Youth Impact Bootcamp LATAM consists of two comprehensive phases.

Phase One: Bootcamp Phase

Programme Period:
24 August 2026 – 16 December 2026

This first phase combines practical entrepreneurship education with continuous collaboration and business development.

Participants will take part in:

  • Live masterclasses
  • Impact Circles
  • Coffee Roulettes
  • Self-paced online learning
  • Practical assignments
  • Business development exercises
  • Peer collaboration sessions

At the end of the Bootcamp Phase, participants will compete in an internal pitch competition.

Following this competition, the Top 8 entrepreneurs will be selected to advance to the next stage.

Phase Two: High Performance Phase

Programme Period:
January – April 2027

The High Performance Phase provides deeper support for the selected entrepreneurs through:

  • Individual coaching
  • Personalized mentoring
  • International visibility
  • Investor exposure
  • Business acceleration
  • Opportunities to pitch before:
    • European Union delegations
    • Programme partners
    • Investors
    • Business stakeholders

What Participants Will Learn

The curriculum covers essential entrepreneurial competencies required to build sustainable impact businesses.

Business Foundations

Participants will strengthen:

  • Business strategy
  • Growth planning
  • Sustainable business models
  • Operational development

Marketing

Founders will learn:

  • Marketing strategy
  • Customer engagement
  • Brand positioning
  • Market expansion

Business Model Canvas & Design Thinking

Sessions focus on:

  • Business model development
  • Innovation
  • Customer-centred product design
  • Problem-solving methodologies

Artificial Intelligence Tools

Participants will explore:

  • AI applications for startups
  • Productivity tools
  • Digital transformation
  • Business automation

ESG and Responsible Business

Experts will introduce:

  • Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) principles
  • Responsible business practices
  • Sustainable entrepreneurship

These sessions are delivered in collaboration with:

  • EY

Legal Frameworks for ESG

Participants will receive legal guidance on:

  • ESG compliance
  • Responsible business governance
  • International legal considerations

These sessions are delivered with:

  • Sidley

Standards and Compliance

Training includes:

  • International business standards
  • Market readiness
  • Quality frameworks

Pitch Training

Entrepreneurs will develop:

  • Investor pitching skills
  • Presentation techniques
  • Storytelling
  • Business communication

Vibe Coding and No-Code Tools

Founders will gain practical exposure to:

  • No-code development
  • Digital product creation
  • Technology tools for entrepreneurs

Additional Digital Courses

In addition to live sessions, participants complete two self-paced online courses.

Investment Readiness

This course helps entrepreneurs:

  • Prepare for fundraising
  • Improve investor communication
  • Understand financing opportunities

Social Impact

Participants learn how to:

  • Measure social impact
  • Evaluate environmental outcomes
  • Communicate impact effectively
  • Build stronger impact strategies

Community-Based Learning Activities

One of the programme’s distinguishing features is its collaborative learning environment.

Impact Circles

Participants join regional peer-learning groups where they:

  • Share business progress
  • Discuss challenges
  • Receive feedback
  • Apply lessons learned

Coffee Roulettes

These one-on-one networking sessions allow entrepreneurs to:

  • Meet fellow founders
  • Exchange experiences
  • Explore collaborations
  • Build professional relationships

Practical Challenges

Throughout the programme, founders complete practical business exercises that reinforce learning through implementation.

Benefits of the Programme

Selected entrepreneurs will receive numerous benefits.

1. Expert-Led Entrepreneurship Training

Access to practical business education delivered by international experts.

2. Personalized Coaching

Top-performing entrepreneurs receive tailored business coaching.

3. International Exposure

Selected participants gain opportunities to present their businesses before:

  • European Union representatives
  • Programme partners
  • Investors
  • International stakeholders

4. Investment Readiness Support

Participants strengthen their ability to attract future funding.

5. Networking Opportunities

Connect with:

  • Fellow entrepreneurs
  • Industry experts
  • Investors
  • International organizations

6. Community Membership

Every participant joins the Ye! Community, a global entrepreneurial network that provides:

  • Networking opportunities
  • Future business opportunities
  • Continued engagement after the programme
  • Peer support

7. Practical Business Growth

The programme focuses on helping entrepreneurs achieve measurable improvements in:

  • Business performance
  • Market readiness
  • Operational systems
  • Growth strategies

Priority Themes

The programme prioritizes startups working in sectors aligned with the European Union Global Gateway investment priorities in Latin America, including:

  • Renewable energy
  • Green hydrogen
  • Sustainable agriculture
  • Forestry
  • Biodiversity
  • Green economy
  • Digital innovation
  • Inclusive development

Founders building solutions connected to flagship projects in their respective countries are especially encouraged to apply.

Who Should Apply?

The Youth Impact Bootcamp LATAM is ideal for:

  • Social entrepreneurs
  • Environmental innovators
  • Startup founders
  • Green business owners
  • Technology entrepreneurs
  • Young business leaders
  • Early-stage startups seeking growth

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must satisfy all of the following requirements:

  • At least one co-founder must be younger than 35 years old.
  • The company must be legally registered, active, and operate as a for-profit business.
  • The business must already exist; the programme is not intended for business ideas alone.
  • The startup must generate measurable social and/or environmental impact.
  • The company must be based in:
    • Argentina
    • Colombia
    • El Salvador
    • Mexico
  • Applicants must be available to dedicate approximately two hours per week to programme activities.

Application Requirements

Before beginning the application, applicants should prepare:

  • Personal identification document
  • Company registration certificate (where applicable)
  • Basic company information
  • Details about:
    • Business model
    • Business activities
    • Social or environmental impact

Applicants should note:

  • The application takes approximately 20–30 minutes to complete.
  • The application must be completed in one sitting.
  • Applicants may download a sample list of questions before beginning the application.

How to Apply

Applicants should:

  1. Join the Ye! Community.
  2. Complete the Online Application Form.
  3. Submit all requested information before the application deadline.

Programme Timeline

  • Applications Open: Currently Open
  • Application Deadline: 19 July 2026
  • Programme Starts: 24 August 2026
  • Bootcamp Phase: 24 August – 16 December 2026
  • Pitch Competition: December 2026
  • High Performance Phase: January – April 2027

Contact Information

For programme or application questions, applicants may contact:

Email: youthandtrade@intracen.org

Why You Should Apply

The Youth Impact Bootcamp LATAM is much more than a startup training programme.

It provides founders with:

  • International mentorship
  • Practical entrepreneurial education
  • Peer collaboration
  • Business acceleration
  • Investment readiness preparation
  • Exposure to investors
  • Access to the European Union innovation ecosystem
  • Membership in a global entrepreneurial network

Whether you are scaling a climate startup, building a digital solution, or creating a socially impactful enterprise, this programme offers valuable resources to help accelerate your business journey.

Visit HERE to learn more about the Youth Impact Bootcamp LATAM 2026.

Conclusion

The Youth Impact Bootcamp LATAM 2026 represents one of the most comprehensive entrepreneurship acceleration opportunities currently available for young founders in Latin America. By combining expert-led training, practical business development, investment readiness, peer collaboration, personalized coaching, and international exposure, the programme equips entrepreneurs with the knowledge, networks, and confidence needed to grow sustainable businesses that address pressing social and environmental challenges.

If you are a young entrepreneur in Argentina, Colombia, El Salvador, or Mexico leading an innovative business with measurable impact, this programme provides a unique opportunity to strengthen your venture, connect with global partners, and position your startup for future investment and international expansion. Interested founders should prepare their application materials and submit their applications before 19 July 2026 to be considered for this highly competitive programme.

Discover more global Fellowship and Accelerator programs on OFY.

The post Youth Impact Bootcamp LATAM 2026: Fully Funded International Startup Accelerator for Young Social Entrepreneurs in Latin America appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNICEF Knowledge Management Analyst Job Opportunity: Advance Digital Safety, Child Rights, and Cybersecurity Through Global Knowledge Leadership
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
UNICEF is seeking a talented and highly motivated Knowledge Management Analyst to join its Digital Impact Division in an exciting role focused on strengthening digital safety, cybersecurity awareness, knowledge management, advocacy, research, and child rights in the digital age. This position offers professionals an opportunity to contribute to UNICEF’s growing Digital Guardrails Agenda, helping develop […]

The post UNICEF Knowledge Management Analyst Job Opportunity: Advance Digital Safety, Child Rights, and Cybersecurity Through Global Knowledge Leadership appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
UNICEF is seeking a talented and highly motivated Knowledge Management Analyst to join its Digital Impact Division in an exciting role focused on strengthening digital safety, cybersecurity awareness, knowledge management, advocacy, research, and child rights in the digital age.

This position offers professionals an opportunity to contribute to UNICEF’s growing Digital Guardrails Agenda, helping develop innovative knowledge products, learning programmes, advocacy campaigns, research outputs, and strategic partnerships that promote safe, secure, and rights-respecting digital ecosystems for children worldwide.

If you are passionate about digital transformation, cybersecurity, knowledge management, international development, and protecting children’s rights online, this position provides a unique opportunity to work alongside international experts while making a global impact.

About the UNICEF Knowledge Management Analyst Position

The Knowledge Management Analyst is far more than a traditional training or communications role. Rather than focusing solely on developing educational materials, the successful candidate will help UNICEF connect, organize, translate, and scale knowledge across multiple technical and policy areas.

Working under the supervision of the Knowledge Management Officer, the successful candidate will support initiatives that combine:

  • Knowledge management
  • Information security awareness
  • Cybersecurity education
  • Research and evidence generation
  • Digital policy
  • Child rights advocacy
  • Behaviour change communication
  • International partnerships
  • Learning programme development

The position bridges technical expertise and public communication by transforming complex digital governance and cybersecurity concepts into practical tools, engaging learning experiences, compelling advocacy materials, and evidence-based policy resources.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Digital technologies continue transforming how children learn, communicate, and access services. At the same time, emerging digital risks—including cybersecurity threats, data privacy concerns, misinformation, and online harms—require coordinated global responses.

UNICEF’s Digital Guardrails initiative seeks to strengthen digital ecosystems that protect children’s rights while promoting responsible innovation.

As a Knowledge Management Analyst, you will contribute directly to these efforts by helping UNICEF:

  • Build stronger cybersecurity awareness among staff.
  • Develop evidence-based digital policies.
  • Produce practical knowledge resources.
  • Strengthen advocacy for child rights in digital environments.
  • Support governments and international partners.
  • Improve digital resilience across UNICEF programmes.

Purpose of the Role

The position supports the Digital Impact Division by designing and implementing integrated knowledge management and learning solutions that strengthen UNICEF’s Digital Guardrails agenda.

The role contributes to four major areas:

  1. Strengthening information security awareness and behavioural change.
  2. Supporting advocacy and communication around digital safety.
  3. Advancing research on cybersecurity, digital governance, and child rights.
  4. Enhancing collaboration across UNICEF, governments, UN agencies, and international partners.

Key Responsibilities

1. Learning and Information Security Awareness

The successful candidate will:

  • Design information security awareness programmes.
  • Develop mandatory and targeted learning initiatives.
  • Support multilingual learning delivery.
  • Improve cybersecurity awareness across UNICEF.
  • Introduce innovative learning experiences that encourage behavioural change.
  • Continuously evaluate and improve learning programmes.

2. Knowledge Products and Communications

The role involves producing high-quality communication resources, including:

  • Guidance documents
  • Policy briefs
  • Toolkits
  • Communication assets
  • Learning materials
  • Educational resources

The analyst will simplify highly technical concepts into accessible content suitable for a wide range of audiences.

3. Advocacy and Strategic Partnerships

The position contributes to UNICEF’s advocacy efforts by:

  • Developing key messages.
  • Supporting advocacy campaigns.
  • Preparing briefing documents.
  • Creating materials for conferences and events.
  • Producing resources for governments and partners.
  • Supporting collaboration with:
    • UN agencies
    • Government institutions
    • Technical experts
    • International stakeholders

4. Research and Knowledge Generation

Research responsibilities include:

  • Conducting literature reviews.
  • Synthesizing research findings.
  • Drafting analytical reports.
  • Producing evidence-based policy narratives.
  • Supporting research on:
    • Cybersecurity
    • Data governance
    • Digital risks
    • Child online protection
    • Digital safeguards

5. Data Monitoring and Programme Improvement

The analyst will also support programme monitoring through:

  • Training completion analysis.
  • Campaign engagement tracking.
  • Dashboard development.
  • Reporting and visualization.
  • Performance monitoring.
  • Strategic decision support.

Expected Deliverables

Throughout the assignment, the Knowledge Management Analyst will contribute to:

Quarterly Deliverables

  • Improved learning and awareness initiatives.
  • Information security training.
  • Phishing simulation programmes.
  • Information Security Awareness bulletins.
  • Communication materials.
  • Knowledge products.
  • Advocacy campaigns.
  • Programme dashboards.
  • Monitoring reports.

Ongoing Deliverables

  • Literature reviews.
  • Research reports.
  • Policy briefs.
  • Partnership materials.
  • Event resources.
  • Advocacy messaging.
  • Strategic analytical outputs.

Education Requirements

Applicants must possess:

Required

  • A bachelor’s degree in:
    • Information Management
    • Digital Studies
    • Computer Science
    • International Development
    • Human Rights
    • Or another closely related field.

Preferred

  • A Master’s degree or equivalent qualification in a related discipline.

Professional Experience

Applicants should have a minimum of two years of relevant experience demonstrating expertise in one or more of the following:

  • Cybersecurity
  • Digital governance
  • Digital policy
  • Child rights
  • Human rights
  • Research
  • Knowledge management
  • Communications

Candidates should also demonstrate experience in:

  • Data analysis
  • Data visualization
  • Research synthesis
  • Knowledge product development
  • Learning content creation
  • Communication material development

Desired Skills

UNICEF is particularly interested in candidates who demonstrate:

  • Intellectual curiosity.
  • Strong analytical thinking.
  • Excellent writing abilities.
  • Initiative and independence.
  • Creativity.
  • Adaptability.
  • Strong collaboration skills.
  • Interdisciplinary thinking.
  • Experience using AI tools for research or prototyping.
  • Familiarity with e-learning platforms.
  • Experience with digital communication tools.
  • Knowledge of visual content creation tools.

Who Is the Ideal Candidate?

The organization is seeking professionals who are:

  • Curious and eager to learn.
  • Comfortable working across multiple disciplines.
  • Strong problem-solvers.
  • Able to manage changing priorities.
  • Creative communicators.
  • Passionate about child rights.
  • Interested in digital transformation.
  • Motivated by global impact.

Why Join UNICEF?

Working with UNICEF provides an opportunity to:

  • Contribute to global digital transformation initiatives.
  • Protect children’s rights in digital spaces.
  • Collaborate with international experts.
  • Work across governments and UN agencies.
  • Develop innovative knowledge products.
  • Influence digital policy and cybersecurity awareness.
  • Build expertise in research, advocacy, learning, and international development.

Application Information

Interested applicants should prepare a professional application highlighting:

  • Relevant education.
  • Professional experience.
  • Research and analytical skills.
  • Knowledge management experience.
  • Communication abilities.
  • Interest in digital transformation and child rights.
  • Experience with cybersecurity, AI tools, or digital policy where applicable.

Apply Here

Application Deadline: Not specified in the announcement. Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible once the official vacancy is available.

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post UNICEF Knowledge Management Analyst Job Opportunity: Advance Digital Safety, Child Rights, and Cybersecurity Through Global Knowledge Leadership appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: NYC Men Heal Scholarship 2026: Financial Support, Mentorship, and Career Development for Future Behavioral Health Professionals in New York City
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The NYC Men Heal Scholarship 2026 is now accepting applications from graduate students pursuing careers in behavioral health. This exciting initiative provides financial assistance, academic support, professional development, mentorship, networking opportunities, and career preparation for aspiring mental health professionals studying in New York City. Jointly developed by the NYC Young Men’s Initiative (YMI) under the […]

The post NYC Men Heal Scholarship 2026: Financial Support, Mentorship, and Career Development for Future Behavioral Health Professionals in New York City appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The NYC Men Heal Scholarship 2026 is now accepting applications from graduate students pursuing careers in behavioral health. This exciting initiative provides financial assistance, academic support, professional development, mentorship, networking opportunities, and career preparation for aspiring mental health professionals studying in New York City.

Jointly developed by the NYC Young Men’s Initiative (YMI) under the Mayor’s Office of Equity & Racial Justice (MOERJ), the Mayor’s Office of Community Mental Health (OCMH), the Human Resources Administration (HRA), with additional support from the Mayor’s Office for Economic Opportunity (NYCO), NYC Men Heal aims to strengthen New York City’s behavioral health workforce while supporting students throughout their graduate education.

The application deadline is August 10, 2026.

About the NYC Men Heal Program

Behavioral health professionals continue to play a vital role in improving mental health services and supporting communities across New York City. NYC Men Heal was created to increase the number of qualified professionals entering the behavioral health workforce while helping graduate students overcome financial, academic, and professional barriers.

The Fall 2026 pilot cohort will support approximately 20 to 25 scholars through a comprehensive development program designed to prepare participants for long-term careers in behavioral health.

The initiative combines:

  • Financial support
  • Academic assistance
  • Professional mentoring
  • Career coaching
  • Networking opportunities
  • Licensure preparation
  • Leadership development
  • Workforce pathways

Rather than simply offering funding, the program invests in the long-term success of participants by connecting them with experienced professionals and employment opportunities after graduation.

Program Partners

NYC Men Heal is a collaborative initiative involving several New York City agencies and organizations, including:

  • NYC Young Men’s Initiative (YMI)
  • Mayor’s Office of Equity & Racial Justice (MOERJ)
  • Mayor’s Office of Community Mental Health (OCMH)
  • Human Resources Administration (HRA)
  • Mayor’s Office for Economic Opportunity (NYCO)

The program also works closely with the Career Pathways Training (CPT) initiative administered by the 1199SEIU Training and Employment Funds (TEF).

Priority Consideration

Priority admission will be given to applicants currently enrolled in the Career Pathways Training (CPT) program.

However, students who are not enrolled in CPT are still encouraged to apply and may be admitted depending on available program capacity.

Benefits of the NYC Men Heal Scholarship

Selected scholars will receive a comprehensive package of academic and professional support that includes:

1. Financial Assistance

Participants receive financial support to help reduce educational expenses while completing graduate studies.

2. Academic Support

Students receive guidance and resources that promote academic success throughout their graduate education.

3. Mentorship

Participants are matched with professionals who can provide career guidance, encouragement, and practical advice.

4. Career Coaching

The program prepares scholars for successful careers in behavioral health through career planning, interview preparation, resume development, and professional growth opportunities.

5. Networking Opportunities

Scholars gain access to valuable professional networks within New York City’s behavioral health community.

6. Licensure Preparation

Participants receive support that helps them prepare for professional licensing requirements.

7. Professional Skill Development

The initiative offers culturally grounded programming and practical skills training that strengthen participants’ readiness for employment.

8. Employment Pathways

One of the program’s primary objectives is helping graduates transition into long-term careers within the behavioral health workforce.

Eligible Degree Programs

Applicants must currently be pursuing one of the following graduate degrees:

  • Social Work
  • Mental Health Counseling

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify, applicants must:

  • Be at least 18 years old.
  • Be currently enrolled in a graduate program at an eligible college or university located in New York City.
  • Maintain a cumulative GPA of at least 2.0.
  • Be pursuing a graduate degree in Social Work or Mental Health Counseling.
  • Demonstrate a commitment to working in the behavioral health field after graduation.
  • Submit a complete application before the deadline.

Participating Universities

Applicants may currently be enrolled at institutions such as:

  • Adelphi University
  • Alfred State College
  • Binghamton University
  • College of Staten Island
  • Columbia University
  • Fordham University
  • Hunter College
  • Lehman College
  • Long Island University
  • Mercy College
  • New York University
  • Touro College
  • Yeshiva University

Students attending other eligible graduate institutions in New York City may also apply.

Information Required During Application

Applicants should be prepared to provide:

  • Full name
  • School email
  • Personal email
  • Race and ethnicity (optional demographic information)
  • Gender identity
  • Confirmation of age
  • CPT enrollment status
  • University information
  • Expected graduation year
  • Current GPA
  • Graduate degree program
  • Preferred support services
  • Career goals after graduation

Program Commitment

Applicants must affirm their intention to pursue employment in the behavioral health field after completing their graduate education.

This commitment reflects the initiative’s goal of expanding New York City’s behavioral health workforce.

Equal Opportunity Statement

NYC Men Heal welcomes applications from all eligible students.

The initiative does not discriminate on the basis of:

  • Race
  • Gender
  • National origin
  • Or any other characteristic protected by law

The program is committed to providing equal opportunities for all qualified applicants.

Application Deadline

Applications must be submitted no later than August 10, 2026.

Late submissions may not be considered.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should complete the official NYC Men Heal online application form before the deadline.

Applicants should ensure that all required information is completed accurately and that they clearly describe their career goals and interest in working within the behavioral health profession.

Apply Here

Discover more global scholarship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post NYC Men Heal Scholarship 2026: Financial Support, Mentorship, and Career Development for Future Behavioral Health Professionals in New York City appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: EU Civil Society Fund Plus (CSF+) Round Two 2026: Apply for Grants, Capacity Building, and Networking Opportunities for Local and Grassroots CSOs in Ethiopia
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The Civil Society Fund Plus (CSF+) Programme, financed by the European Union (EU) and implemented by Christian Aid together with its national co-beneficiaries, has officially launched its Round Two Call for Expression of Interest (EOI) for local and grassroots Civil Society Organizations (CSOs) in Ethiopia. This funding opportunity seeks to strengthen the capacity, sustainability, and […]

The post EU Civil Society Fund Plus (CSF+) Round Two 2026: Apply for Grants, Capacity Building, and Networking Opportunities for Local and Grassroots CSOs in Ethiopia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Civil Society Fund Plus (CSF+) Programme, financed by the European Union (EU) and implemented by Christian Aid together with its national co-beneficiaries, has officially launched its Round Two Call for Expression of Interest (EOI) for local and grassroots Civil Society Organizations (CSOs) in Ethiopia.

This funding opportunity seeks to strengthen the capacity, sustainability, and influence of smaller and emerging CSOs that are working to improve governance, democracy, peacebuilding, human rights, social inclusion, and community development. Through financial support, organizational capacity strengthening, and networking opportunities, the programme aims to empower civil society organizations to become stronger and more credible actors in promoting good governance and sustainable development.

The programme is inviting eligible organizations operating within the target regions to submit Expressions of Interest before the application deadline.

Application Deadline: Tuesday, 4 August 2026, before 17:00 (5:00 PM) local time.

About the Civil Society Fund Plus (CSF+) Programme

The Civil Society Fund Plus (CSF+) is a four-year programme funded primarily by the European Union to strengthen civil society organizations in Ethiopia by promoting an enabling civic environment, democratic participation, inclusive governance, and constructive dialogue between policymakers and civil society actors.

The programme officially commenced on 1 February 2025 and will run until 31 January 2029.

Christian Aid is implementing the programme in partnership with three Ethiopian national civil society organizations:

  • Ethiopian Civil Society Organizations Forum (ECSF)
  • Civil Society Resource Center (CSRC)
  • Chura Abugida Artistic Association (CAAA)

Together, these organizations are working to build stronger, more resilient, and more effective local civil society organizations capable of addressing the needs of marginalized communities while contributing to national development priorities.

Programme Coverage

The CSF+ Programme is currently being implemented in the following areas:

  • Addis Ababa City Administration
  • Central Ethiopia Regional State
  • South Ethiopia Regional State
  • Tigray Regional State

Eligible organizations should be operating within these target locations.

Programme Funding

The programme has a total value of:

€4,046,471

Funding contributions include:

  • 85% financed by the European Union
  • 15% financed by Christian Aid

Programme Targets

The CSF+ Programme seeks to create long-term impact by supporting hundreds of civil society organizations and communities.

The programme aims to:

  • Provide direct sub-grants to 60 local and grassroots CSOs
  • Deliver tailored organizational capacity strengthening to 120 CSOs
  • Support approximately 430 CSOs indirectly through networking and collaboration activities
  • Enable participating organizations to reach approximately 120,000 community members
  • Prioritize women, youth, and persons with disabilities as key beneficiaries

During the first funding round:

  • 20 local and grassroots organizations received grants
  • Project implementation began in March 2026

Round Two now seeks to identify an additional:

  • 20 local and grassroots CSOs

Purpose of the Call for Expression of Interest

The second-round Expression of Interest is designed to identify promising local organizations that can implement community-driven initiatives addressing governance, democratic participation, social inclusion, peacebuilding, and sustainable development.

The programme particularly seeks organizations working with marginalized communities while promoting citizen participation and accountability.

What Successful Applicants Will Receive

Selected organizations will benefit from several forms of support.

1. Financial Support

Successful organizations will receive sub-grants to implement community-based initiatives aligned with the programme’s thematic priorities.

Funding will support projects that directly benefit local communities while strengthening civil society participation.

2. Tailored Capacity Strengthening

Beyond funding, participating organizations will receive customized organizational development support, including:

  • Training programmes
  • Workshops
  • Technical assistance
  • Organizational development resources
  • Capacity-building activities designed to improve long-term sustainability and effectiveness

3. Networking Opportunities

The programme will also facilitate collaboration among civil society organizations through:

  • Knowledge-sharing events
  • Strategic partnerships
  • Learning exchanges
  • Networking forums
  • Collaborative advocacy opportunities

These activities are intended to strengthen cooperation among CSOs while creating a stronger enabling environment for civil society.

Priority Project Areas

The programme welcomes projects contributing to one or more of the following thematic areas:

Good Governance

Projects that:

  • Strengthen participatory governance
  • Promote accountability
  • Encourage citizen participation
  • Improve transparency

Civic Space

Initiatives that:

  • Protect civic freedoms
  • Create enabling environments for CSOs
  • Strengthen independent civil society

Governance and Social Inclusion

Projects promoting:

  • Inclusive decision-making
  • Equal participation
  • Community engagement
  • Social justice

Youth Empowerment

Activities that:

  • Strengthen youth leadership
  • Increase youth participation
  • Promote youth voice and agency

Peacebuilding and Conflict Resolution

Projects focused on:

  • Conflict prevention
  • Dialogue
  • Peacebuilding
  • Community reconciliation
  • Social cohesion

Women’s Rights and Leadership

Activities supporting:

  • Women’s empowerment
  • Gender equality
  • Women’s leadership
  • Equal participation

Preventing Irregular Migration

Projects that address the drivers of illegal migration while creating local opportunities.

Social Accountability

Initiatives promoting accountability in delivering:

  • Public services
  • Community services
  • Government programmes

Who Can Apply?

The programme welcomes applications from:

Local and Grassroots Civil Society Organizations

Organizations that:

  • Have operational experience within target regions
  • Maintain a physical office or operational presence in the region where they are applying

Networks and Associations

Organizations representing multiple civil society organizations with shared interests.

CSO Networks

Networks working to:

  • Promote collaboration
  • Encourage knowledge sharing
  • Advocate for enabling environments for civil society

These may operate within:

  • Addis Ababa
  • Target regional states

Priority Applicants

The programme particularly encourages applications from organizations led by:

  • Women
  • Young people
  • Persons with disabilities

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants should carefully review the official guidelines before applying.

Eligible organizations should demonstrate:

  • Operational experience
  • Physical presence within target areas
  • Commitment to community development
  • Alignment with programme thematic priorities

How to Apply

Applicants should follow these steps carefully.

Step 1: Download the Application Documents

Download:

  • Expression of Interest Application Form (Annex II)
  • EOI Guidelines (Annex III)
  • Declaration Form

The documents are available in:

  • English
  • Amharic
  • Tigrigna

Learn more and Apply Here

Step 2: Read the Guidelines

Carefully review:

  • Eligibility criteria
  • Application procedures
  • Required documentation
  • Submission instructions

Step 3: Complete the Application

Fill out:

  • Expression of Interest Form
  • Declaration Form

Attach all required supporting documents.

Step 4: Submit Your Application

Applicants may submit their application through either:

Email

Send completed documents to:

eucsfplusapplicationround2@christian-aid.org

OR

Hand Delivery / Postal Submission

Submit to the physical addresses listed in:

Annex III, Section 7.4, Table 1

Important Application Deadline

All applications must be received no later than:

Tuesday, 4 August 2026

Before 17:00 (5:00 PM) Local Time

Late applications will not be considered.

Why Organizations Should Apply

The Civil Society Fund Plus Programme offers much more than grant funding. Successful organizations will gain long-term institutional support through technical assistance, networking opportunities, and organizational development. This holistic approach helps local and grassroots organizations become stronger, more sustainable, and better positioned to influence governance processes while delivering meaningful impact to underserved communities.

Organizations working in governance, democracy, peacebuilding, women’s empowerment, youth development, disability inclusion, and social accountability stand to benefit significantly from participating in this EU-funded initiative. By combining financial resources with capacity development and collaborative networking, the programme strengthens the overall civil society ecosystem in Ethiopia and empowers organizations to become credible partners in advancing inclusive development and democratic engagement.

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post EU Civil Society Fund Plus (CSF+) Round Two 2026: Apply for Grants, Capacity Building, and Networking Opportunities for Local and Grassroots CSOs in Ethiopia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNITAR Youth Leaders for World Tsunami Awareness Day 2026 Online Course: Free United Nations Disaster Risk Reduction Training for Young People in Asia-Pacific
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The United Nations Institute for Training and Research (UNITAR) is now accepting applications for the Youth Leaders for World Tsunami Awareness Day 2026 Online Course, a fully funded online learning programme designed to empower young people across the Asia-Pacific region with essential knowledge and practical skills in disaster risk reduction, disaster preparedness, climate resilience, and […]

The post UNITAR Youth Leaders for World Tsunami Awareness Day 2026 Online Course: Free United Nations Disaster Risk Reduction Training for Young People in Asia-Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Institute for Training and Research (UNITAR) is now accepting applications for the Youth Leaders for World Tsunami Awareness Day 2026 Online Course, a fully funded online learning programme designed to empower young people across the Asia-Pacific region with essential knowledge and practical skills in disaster risk reduction, disaster preparedness, climate resilience, and sustainable development.

This unique four-week online programme will take place from 1 to 30 September 2026 and is offered completely free of charge thanks to the generous support of the Government and the People of Japan.

The programme brings together young leaders from 14 Asia-Pacific countries to learn from disaster experts, engage with peers from across the region, and explore practical strategies for building safer, more resilient communities.

Participants who successfully complete the course will receive an official UNITAR Certificate of Participation, enhancing their academic and professional profiles while demonstrating their commitment to disaster resilience and sustainable development.

About UNITAR

Established in 1965, the United Nations Institute for Training and Research (UNITAR) serves as the dedicated training arm of the United Nations.

UNITAR strengthens knowledge and institutional capacity through innovative learning programmes, research, and strategic partnerships that support the implementation of the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development and other global development initiatives.

Its programmes equip individuals, governments, and organizations with practical skills to address today’s global challenges.

About the Youth Leaders for World Tsunami Awareness Day 2026 Online Course

The Youth Leaders for World Tsunami Awareness Day Online Course is an international capacity-building initiative aimed at educating young people about disaster preparedness and resilience while encouraging them to become active contributors to safer communities.

The 2026 edition also commemorates the 15th anniversary of the Great East Japan Earthquake and Tsunami, providing participants with an opportunity to study Japan’s disaster preparedness experience alongside lessons learned from disasters across the Asia-Pacific region.

The programme emphasizes youth leadership by encouraging participants to reflect on how disaster risk reduction principles can be applied within their schools, universities, organizations, and local communities.

Programme Duration

The course will run from:

1 September 2026 – 30 September 2026

Participants will spend approximately 1–2 hours per week completing course activities.

Learning Format

The programme is delivered entirely online in English.

Participants will benefit from a flexible learning experience that includes:

  • Self-paced online learning modules
  • Live webinars with international experts
  • Interactive discussions
  • Online youth networking community
  • Practical learning activities
  • Case studies
  • Videos
  • Interactive quizzes
  • Real-world disaster preparedness examples

The course can be accessed using:

  • Smartphones
  • Tablets
  • Laptop computers
  • Desktop computers

What Participants Will Learn

Throughout the programme, participants will explore a wide range of important topics related to disaster risk reduction and resilience.

Key learning areas include:

Disaster Risk Reduction Fundamentals

Participants will gain a strong understanding of:

  • Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR)
  • The Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction
  • The Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)

Lessons from the Great East Japan Earthquake and Tsunami

The course examines:

  • The causes and impacts of the 2011 disaster
  • Recovery efforts
  • Preparedness lessons
  • Community resilience

Disaster Preparedness Across Asia-Pacific

Participants will study:

  • Regional disaster experiences
  • National preparedness initiatives
  • Community-based resilience strategies

Youth Leadership

The programme highlights the important role that young people play in:

  • Community preparedness
  • Public awareness
  • Disaster education
  • Emergency response
  • Local resilience initiatives

Early Warning and Emergency Preparedness

Participants will learn about:

  • Early warning systems
  • Evacuation planning
  • Emergency response strategies
  • Risk communication

Climate Change and Disaster Risk

The programme explores:

  • Climate-related disasters
  • Climate resilience
  • Adaptation strategies
  • Disaster prevention

Building Resilient Communities

Participants will examine concepts including:

  • Inclusive resilience
  • Sustainable recovery
  • “Build Back Better”
  • Community engagement

Benefits of the Programme

Participants selected for the programme will receive numerous benefits, including:

  • Free participation with no tuition fees
  • Training delivered by the United Nations Institute for Training and Research (UNITAR)
  • Official UNITAR Certificate of Participation upon successful completion
  • Flexible online learning format
  • Live webinars with international experts
  • Networking opportunities with youth leaders from across Asia-Pacific
  • Practical knowledge in disaster preparedness and resilience
  • Increased understanding of climate change and disaster risk reduction
  • Exposure to Japanese disaster preparedness experience
  • Enhanced leadership and community engagement skills

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be between 14 and 25 years of age
  • Be a national of or currently reside in one of the participating countries
  • Have an interest in disaster risk reduction, resilience, sustainable development, or related fields
  • Be able to participate in English
  • Commit approximately 1–2 hours per week during the programme

Participating Countries

Eligible applicants must come from one of the following countries:

  • Brunei Darussalam
  • Cambodia
  • China
  • Fiji
  • Federated States of Micronesia
  • Indonesia
  • Japan
  • Lao PDR
  • Malaysia
  • Philippines
  • Republic of Korea
  • Singapore
  • Thailand
  • Viet Nam

Certificate

Participants who successfully complete all programme requirements will receive an official UNITAR Certificate of Participation.

This certificate demonstrates knowledge of disaster risk reduction, climate resilience, and sustainable development and can strengthen future academic, volunteer, scholarship, and career applications.

Why You Should Apply

The UNITAR Youth Leaders for World Tsunami Awareness Day 2026 Online Course provides a unique opportunity to gain internationally recognized training from a United Nations institution while connecting with young leaders across Asia-Pacific.

Participants will develop practical knowledge that can be applied in schools, universities, local communities, and future careers related to disaster management, climate resilience, public policy, sustainable development, and humanitarian work.

Since the programme is offered entirely online and free of charge, it is accessible to motivated young people regardless of financial background.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should complete the official online application form before the application deadline.

Application Deadline: 14 August 2026 (11:59 p.m. JST / UTC+9)

Apply here

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here 
  • More global short courses opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post UNITAR Youth Leaders for World Tsunami Awareness Day 2026 Online Course: Free United Nations Disaster Risk Reduction Training for Young People in Asia-Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: OSCE Cross-Dimensional Work Areas Internship 2026–2027 in Vienna and Prague: Gain International Experience in Peace, Security, Human Rights, and Global Development
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe (OSCE) is now accepting applications for its Cross-Dimensional Work Areas Internship Programme 2026–2027. This highly competitive international internship provides students and recent graduates with an exceptional opportunity to gain practical experience while contributing to one of the world’s largest regional security organizations. Hosted by the OSCE Secretariat […]

The post OSCE Cross-Dimensional Work Areas Internship 2026–2027 in Vienna and Prague: Gain International Experience in Peace, Security, Human Rights, and Global Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe (OSCE) is now accepting applications for its Cross-Dimensional Work Areas Internship Programme 2026–2027. This highly competitive international internship provides students and recent graduates with an exceptional opportunity to gain practical experience while contributing to one of the world’s largest regional security organizations.

Hosted by the OSCE Secretariat in Vienna, Austria, with additional internship opportunities available in Prague, Czech Republic, the programme offers participants hands-on experience across a wide range of international policy areas including peacebuilding, human rights, governance, migration, gender equality, cybersecurity, counter-terrorism, youth engagement, and economic development.

The internship lasts between 2 and 6 months, allowing interns to develop professional skills while working alongside international experts from more than 57 participating States.

About the OSCE

The Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe (OSCE) is the world’s largest regional security organization, bringing together 57 participating States across North America, Europe, and Asia.

Its mission is to promote:

  • Peace
  • Stability
  • Security
  • Democracy
  • Human rights
  • Good governance
  • Economic cooperation
  • Environmental sustainability

The organization addresses modern security challenges through an integrated approach that combines political dialogue with practical action.

Today’s issues—including migration, human trafficking, violent extremism, cyber security, gender equality, education, and youth participation—cut across traditional sectors, making cross-dimensional cooperation essential.

About the Cross-Dimensional Internship Programme

The OSCE Secretariat is recruiting qualified interns to support its Cross-Dimensional Work Areas.

Internships are available for 2 to 6 months, depending on departmental needs.

Participants work directly with experienced international professionals while contributing to projects that support security, development, and international cooperation.

Interns become part of multicultural teams and gain first-hand exposure to international diplomacy and policy implementation.

Benefits of the OSCE Internship

Successful interns will enjoy numerous professional and learning opportunities, including:

  • Practical work experience within a leading international organization
  • Exposure to international diplomacy and peacebuilding
  • Professional mentoring from experienced experts
  • Networking with professionals from around the world
  • Opportunity to collaborate with colleagues from diverse cultures
  • Development of research, communication, and policy skills
  • Experience working in multicultural and multilingual environments
  • Understanding of international governance and security issues
  • Participation in meaningful projects with global impact
  • Opportunity to build an international professional network

Although the internship is unpaid, eligible interns who are non-residents at their duty station may receive a contribution toward living costs from the receiving office.

The OSCE also provides:

  • Life insurance
  • Disability insurance
  • Visa support letters where required

Internship Locations

Selected candidates may be assigned to:

  • Vienna, Austria
  • Prague, Czech Republic

Start dates depend on the operational needs of the hiring office.

Internship Duration

The internship lasts between:

  • Minimum: 2 months
  • Maximum: 6 months

Please note that an individual cannot complete more than 6 cumulative months of internship within the OSCE.

Working Arrangements

The internship is generally:

  • Full-time (40 hours per week)

However, part-time arrangements may be approved in exceptional circumstances.

Typical Responsibilities

Interns may contribute to a wide variety of activities depending on the department.

Responsibilities may include:

Research and Policy Development

  • Draft research papers
  • Prepare policy reports
  • Develop concept notes
  • Produce briefing papers
  • Draft speeches
  • Prepare newsletters and presentations

Project Management

Interns may assist with:

  • Project planning
  • Budget preparation
  • Donor reporting
  • Monitoring implementation
  • Preparing project documentation

Legal and Analytical Support

Responsibilities may include:

  • Legal research
  • Reviewing legal documents
  • Preparing legal opinions
  • Conducting thematic research
  • Media monitoring
  • Survey implementation
  • Financial analysis

Communications and Public Outreach

Interns may support:

  • Website updates
  • Publications
  • Social media content
  • Booklets
  • Information leaflets
  • Audio-visual communication materials

Event Management

Interns frequently assist in organizing:

  • Conferences
  • Workshops
  • Press briefings
  • Roundtables
  • Regional meetings
  • Official events

Meeting Support

Tasks include:

  • Attending official meetings
  • Taking minutes
  • Preparing official records
  • Drafting meeting reports

Administrative Support

Departments may require assistance in:

  • Human Resources
  • Procurement
  • Financial Services
  • Supply Chain
  • Travel Management
  • Information Technology

Executive Support

Interns may assist senior officials by:

  • Preparing background documents
  • Organizing travel
  • Supporting country visits
  • Coordinating logistics
  • Communicating with internal and external stakeholders

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be no older than 30 years by the application deadline
  • Have completed at least two years of higher education, or
  • Have graduated within the previous two years
  • Hold a degree in a relevant discipline such as:
    • International Relations
    • Law
    • Human Rights
    • Gender Studies
    • Anthropology
    • Social Sciences
    • Similar fields
  • Demonstrate professional fluency in English (written and spoken)
  • Possess excellent communication skills
  • Be highly motivated and adaptable
  • Be able to work effectively with multicultural teams
  • Demonstrate impartiality and professionalism
  • Understand the importance of gender mainstreaming
  • Be proficient in Microsoft Office applications

Preferred Qualifications

The OSCE also values candidates who have:

  • Previous relevant work experience
  • Experience in multicultural environments
  • Strong research and analytical abilities
  • Excellent drafting skills
  • Organizational and multitasking skills
  • Knowledge of another OSCE language

Financial Information

The internship does not include:

  • Salary
  • Travel reimbursement

However:

  • Non-resident interns may receive a contribution toward living expenses, depending on the receiving office.
  • The OSCE provides life and disability insurance.
  • Interns without appropriate health insurance must join the OSCE group health insurance scheme at their own expense.

Important Notes

Applicants should remember that:

  • The internship does not guarantee future employment.
  • Availability dates should be included in the cover letter.
  • Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
  • The OSCE never charges any application or recruitment fees.
  • The organization is committed to diversity, inclusion, and equal opportunity for all qualified applicants.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should submit their applications through the official OSCE online recruitment portal before the closing date.

Applicants are encouraged to prepare a strong cover letter highlighting their motivation, relevant experience, and availability.

Application Deadline: 31 December 2026

For additional information about internships, fields of work, and the recruitment process, applicants should consult the official OSCE internship resources and recruitment portal.

Apply here  

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Internship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here 

The post OSCE Cross-Dimensional Work Areas Internship 2026–2027 in Vienna and Prague: Gain International Experience in Peace, Security, Human Rights, and Global Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: YLabs Senior Research Specialist (Contractor) 2026: Remote Africa-Based Consultancy Paying Up to USD 55,896 Per Year
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
YLabs is inviting applications for the position of Senior Research Specialist (Contractor), offering experienced African researchers an exciting opportunity to lead innovative research projects that improve the lives of young people across the continent. This fully remote consultancy allows professionals to work from any African country while collaborating with one of the world’s leading youth-centered […]

The post YLabs Senior Research Specialist (Contractor) 2026: Remote Africa-Based Consultancy Paying Up to USD 55,896 Per Year appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
YLabs is inviting applications for the position of Senior Research Specialist (Contractor), offering experienced African researchers an exciting opportunity to lead innovative research projects that improve the lives of young people across the continent.

This fully remote consultancy allows professionals to work from any African country while collaborating with one of the world’s leading youth-centered design and research organizations. The successful candidate will lead high-impact research initiatives, influence strategic decision-making, and contribute to projects focused on youth economic empowerment, education, entrepreneurship, financial inclusion, and innovation.

With an attractive annual consultancy package ranging from USD 35,460 to USD 55,896, this opportunity is ideal for experienced researchers seeking to make meaningful contributions to development across Africa.

About YLabs

YLabs is a globally recognized design and research organization founded in 2014 at the Harvard Innovation Lab. The organization develops technologies and evidence-based solutions that empower young people by increasing their agency, opportunities, and participation in designing solutions to challenges affecting their lives.

YLabs believes that young people should be the architects of solutions to the issues they face. Through participatory and human-centered design approaches, the organization works alongside youth to co-create innovative digital products and services.

Operating from offices in Rwanda and the United States, YLabs has implemented projects across 18 countries, focusing on three primary areas:

  • Optimum health and wellness
  • Improved economic opportunity
  • Climate resilience

The organization combines expertise in:

  • Youth-driven design
  • Digital product development
  • Behavioral science
  • Public health
  • Epidemiology
  • Climate innovation
  • Livelihoods
  • Research and evaluation

About the Senior Research Specialist Position

YLabs is seeking a highly experienced Senior Research Specialist based in Africa to lead strategic research initiatives under its Research, Impact and Innovation Department.

The selected consultant will oversee complex research projects from design through implementation while producing evidence that informs innovation and strategic programming.

This position requires an individual who can work independently, manage multiple projects simultaneously, and generate high-quality research outputs that influence organizational and donor decision-making.

Contract Details

Successful applicants will receive:

  • Independent consultancy contract
  • Contract duration of up to 12 months
  • Expected start date: 1 August 2026
  • Fully remote position
  • Work from anywhere in Africa
  • Travel opportunities throughout Africa for field research
  • Monthly payments through bank transfer upon approved deliverables

Salary

The consultancy offers an annual gross remuneration ranging from:

USD 35,460 – USD 55,896

Compensation will depend on qualifications, experience, and expertise.

Key Responsibilities

The successful consultant will perform a wide range of strategic research and leadership responsibilities.

1. Research Strategy and Leadership

Responsibilities include:

  • Designing and managing large-scale research projects
  • Developing innovative research methodologies
  • Promoting best practices in economic development and youth employment research
  • Collaborating with donors, stakeholders, and regional partners
  • Building research capacity across regional teams
  • Delivering research training and mentorship

2. Quantitative and Qualitative Research

The consultant will:

  • Conduct interviews
  • Facilitate focus groups
  • Lead stakeholder consultations
  • Perform quantitative and qualitative data analysis
  • Analyze large datasets
  • Generate evidence-based recommendations
  • Identify trends and research insights

3. Innovation and Insight Generation

The role involves applying innovative research methodologies such as:

  • Human-Centered Design
  • Participatory Action Research
  • User-centered innovation frameworks

The specialist will also identify emerging opportunities within:

  • Youth entrepreneurship
  • Education
  • Economic development
  • Financial inclusion

Strategic Knowledge Management

The successful candidate will:

  • Prepare comprehensive research reports
  • Produce policy briefs
  • Develop presentations for internal and external audiences
  • Document lessons learned
  • Facilitate knowledge-sharing sessions
  • Build institutional research knowledge systems

Stakeholder Engagement

The consultant will work closely with:

  • Internal YLabs teams
  • Academic institutions
  • Development partners
  • Donors
  • Regional experts
  • Research organizations

Responsibilities also include producing high-quality research outputs that demonstrate project impact for donor reporting.

Monitoring, Evaluation and Learning (MEL)

The successful applicant will collaborate with Monitoring, Evaluation and Learning teams by:

  • Integrating research findings into MEL systems
  • Improving research methodologies
  • Supporting evidence-based learning
  • Strengthening project evaluation frameworks

Project Management

The Senior Research Specialist will also:

  • Manage research timelines
  • Coordinate project deliverables
  • Monitor implementation progress
  • Report findings and recommendations to senior leadership

Required Qualifications

Applicants should:

  • Be currently based in Africa
  • Have experience researching youth economic empowerment
  • Demonstrate adaptability and initiative
  • Work effectively in multicultural environments
  • Communicate research findings to both technical and non-technical audiences
  • Be willing to travel and stay in rural communities for up to seven days

Required Experience

Candidates must have:

  • At least five years of research leadership experience
  • Experience managing donor-funded projects
  • Expertise in qualitative and mixed-methods research
  • Experience recruiting adolescents and youth participants
  • Strong project management skills
  • Proficiency in SPSS, Stata, R, NVivo, or similar software
  • Excellent English communication skills

Knowledge of additional African languages is considered an advantage.

Preferred Qualifications

Preference will be given to candidates with:

  • Master’s degree or PhD in a related discipline
  • Previous research on youth economic empowerment
  • Community-based participatory research experience
  • Experience working with vulnerable populations
  • Knowledge of co-design and rapid prototyping methodologies

Why Apply?

Joining YLabs offers an opportunity to:

  • Lead high-impact research across Africa
  • Influence policy and innovation
  • Work with international development experts
  • Contribute to youth-centered solutions
  • Gain experience in multidisciplinary global research
  • Work remotely while traveling across Africa
  • Receive competitive consultancy compensation
  • Collaborate with global donors and development partners

Diversity and Equal Opportunity

YLabs is committed to creating an inclusive workplace where diversity is valued.

Applications are welcomed from individuals regardless of:

  • Race
  • Ethnicity
  • Religion
  • Gender
  • Sexual orientation
  • Disability
  • Socio-economic background
  • Age
  • Marital status

Employment offers are subject to satisfactory background and criminal record checks.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit their applications through the official YLabs recruitment portal.

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, meaning candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible since the position will remain open until filled.

Application Deadline: No fixed deadline (Applications reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled.)

Apply here 

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here 
  • More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here 

The post YLabs Senior Research Specialist (Contractor) 2026: Remote Africa-Based Consultancy Paying Up to USD 55,896 Per Year appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Research Assistant, Armament and Disarmament at SIPRI 2026 in Sweden | International Peace and Security Research Job Opportunity
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (SIPRI) is inviting applications for the position of Research Assistant within Armament and Disarmament to support its internationally recognized research programmes on military expenditure, arms production, arms trade controls, disarmament, and artificial intelligence governance. This full-time research position offers an exceptional opportunity for early-career researchers and professionals interested in […]

The post Research Assistant, Armament and Disarmament at SIPRI 2026 in Sweden | International Peace and Security Research Job Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (SIPRI) is inviting applications for the position of Research Assistant within Armament and Disarmament to support its internationally recognized research programmes on military expenditure, arms production, arms trade controls, disarmament, and artificial intelligence governance.

This full-time research position offers an exceptional opportunity for early-career researchers and professionals interested in global security, peace studies, defence policy, international relations, and emerging security technologies to contribute to evidence-based research that informs policymakers, academics, international organizations, and the global public.

The successful candidate will join SIPRI’s Armament and Disarmament cluster and contribute to one or more of three major research programmes:

  • Military Expenditure and Arms Production (MILAP)
  • Dual-use and Arms Trade Control (DUAT)
  • Governance of Artificial Intelligence (GOV-AI)

The position is based in Stockholm, Sweden, with an expected starting date in Q4 2026 or as soon as possible thereafter.

The initial contract duration is two years, with the possibility of extension.

Application deadline: 26 July 2026 at 23:59 CEST.

 

About the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (SIPRI)

Established in 1966, the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (SIPRI) is an independent international research institute dedicated to studying security, conflict, peace, and global stability.

SIPRI conducts research and provides reliable data, analysis, and policy recommendations based on publicly available information. Its work supports:

  • Policymakers
  • Researchers
  • International organizations
  • Journalists
  • Civil society organizations
  • The general public

SIPRI is internationally recognized for its objective research, transparency, and commitment to improving understanding of security challenges worldwide.

The institute’s research on armament and disarmament focuses on:

  • The development of military technologies
  • Global arms production
  • International arms transfers
  • Military expenditure trends
  • Conventional weapons
  • Nuclear, chemical, and biological weapons
  • Export control systems
  • Emerging technologies including artificial intelligence and quantum technologies

 

Research Areas Covered by the Position

The selected Research Assistant will support activities across SIPRI’s Armament and Disarmament programmes.

1. Military Expenditure and Arms Production Programme (MILAP)

SIPRI’s Military Expenditure and Arms Production Programme is one of its oldest and most established research areas.

The programme manages important global databases, including:

  • SIPRI Military Expenditure Database
  • SIPRI Arms Industry Database

These databases provide transparent and independently verifiable information covering military spending and arms production across almost every country worldwide.

Responsibilities under MILAP include:

  • Supporting research activities related to:
    • Military expenditure
    • Defence industries
    • Arms production trends
  • Collecting and analyzing data.
  • Drafting research reports and publications.
  • Identifying new information sources and validating data.
  • Supporting:
    • SIPRI Yearbook production
    • Database updates
    • Externally funded research projects
  • Reviewing and fact-checking publications.
  • Proofreading reports and research outputs.
  • Supporting communication with media and external stakeholders.
  • Assisting with:
    • Research events
    • Fundraising activities
    • Project coordination

 

2. Dual-use and Arms Trade Control Programme (DUAT)

The DUAT Programme focuses on strengthening knowledge and international cooperation related to arms trade regulations and dual-use technologies.

The programme examines how countries and international organizations develop and implement:

  • Export control systems
  • Arms trade regulations
  • International agreements
  • Regional security standards

Responsibilities under DUAT include:

  • Conducting research on international and regional arms trade control frameworks.
  • Monitoring trends in export control agreements and implementation.
  • Supporting research publications.
  • Contributing to SIPRI Yearbook activities.
  • Assisting with project dissemination.
  • Supporting seminars, workshops, and awareness-raising activities.
  • Helping organize events and fundraising activities.

 

3. Governance of Artificial Intelligence Programme (GOV-AI)

The Governance of AI Programme explores the relationship between artificial intelligence, security, conflict, and international peace.

The programme examines:

  • How AI technologies may transform warfare.
  • Risks associated with autonomous weapons.
  • AI applications in military environments.
  • Governance frameworks for emerging technologies.
  • International approaches to AI safety and security.

Responsibilities under GOV-AI include:

  • Conducting research on AI-related security challenges.
  • Collecting and analyzing information.
  • Managing and organizing research sources.
  • Supporting externally funded projects.
  • Preparing press reviews.
  • Contributing to research publications and project outputs.
  • Supporting dissemination activities.
  • Helping organize workshops, dialogues, and capacity-building activities.

 

Main Responsibilities of the Research Assistant

Under the supervision of a Programme Director, the Research Assistant will:

  • Support implementation of SIPRI research activities.
  • Conduct qualitative and quantitative research.
  • Collect, analyze, and organize information.
  • Prepare research materials and reports.
  • Contribute to publications and policy outputs.
  • Support international research projects.
  • Participate in workshops, seminars, and expert discussions.
  • Assist with administrative and coordination tasks related to research activities.

The position requires flexibility, professionalism, independence, and the ability to manage multiple priorities.

 

Required Qualifications and Skills

Applicants must demonstrate:

Academic Qualifications

A university degree or degrees in relevant disciplines, including:

  • Political Science
  • International Relations
  • Economics
  • Security Studies
  • Related fields relevant to international peace and security

Research Knowledge

Candidates should have:

  • Strong interest in:
    • Arms control
    • Disarmament
    • Non-proliferation
    • International security issues
  • Experience conducting open-source research.
  • Ability to analyze complex information.
  • Attention to detail and strong research skills.

Professional Skills

Successful candidates should have:

  • Ability to work independently.
  • Strong organizational skills.
  • Effective time management abilities.
  • Ability to work collaboratively within an international team.
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills.

Language Requirements

Applicants must have:

  • Excellent written and spoken English skills.

Additional languages are considered an advantage.

Swedish language knowledge is not required.

Technical Skills

Applicants should have:

  • Very good knowledge of Microsoft Office tools:
    • Word
    • Excel
    • PowerPoint
    • Outlook

Candidates should also be willing and able to quickly learn new research and digital tools.

 

Contract Details and Benefits

Selected candidates will receive:

  • A full-time employment contract.
  • Initial two-year appointment.
  • Possibility of contract extension.
  • Work location in Stockholm, Sweden.
  • Support with work permit and migration processes where applicable.
  • Economy-class travel support to Stockholm for successful candidates.
  • Opportunity to work within a globally recognized peace and security research institute.
  • Exposure to international experts, researchers, policymakers, and global security discussions.

Please note:

  • Moving and relocation expenses are not covered.
  • Salary will be determined individually based on qualifications and professional experience.

 

Application Process

Applicants must submit the following documents in English:

1. Cover Letter

A one-page cover letter explaining:

  • Motivation for applying.
  • Interest in the position.
  • Preferred programme:
    • MILAP
    • DUAT
    • Governance of AI

2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)

The CV should include:

  • Academic qualifications.
  • Professional experience.
  • Publications.
  • Language skills.
  • Relevant research experience.

References will be requested from shortlisted candidates during the recruitment process.

Applications must be submitted through the official SIPRI recruitment platform. Applications sent through other channels will not be considered.

 

Important Dates

  • Application deadline: 26 July 2026, 23:59 CEST
  • Expected interviews: End of August / Early September 2026
  • Expected starting date: Q4 2026 or as soon as possible thereafter

 

Why Apply for This SIPRI Research Assistant Position?

This opportunity is ideal for candidates who want to:

  • Build a career in international peace and security research.
  • Gain experience with globally recognized research databases.
  • Work on issues affecting global stability.
  • Contribute to evidence-based policy discussions.
  • Develop expertise in arms control, military affairs, and emerging technologies.
  • Collaborate with researchers from around the world.

 

How to Apply

Interested candidates can submit their application through the official SIPRI recruitment portal:

 

Apply here

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Research Assistant, Armament and Disarmament at SIPRI 2026 in Sweden | International Peace and Security Research Job Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Communications and Digital Engagement Officer at UNITEE (Brussels, Belgium): Apply for a European Communications Career Supporting Entrepreneurship, Inclusion, and EU Projects
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
If you are passionate about communications, digital storytelling, European cooperation, entrepreneurship, and creating meaningful social impact, the Communications and Digital Engagement Officer position at UNITEE – New European Business Confederation offers an outstanding opportunity to begin or advance your career in the heart of Europe. Based in Brussels, Belgium, this full-time role is designed for […]

The post Communications and Digital Engagement Officer at UNITEE (Brussels, Belgium): Apply for a European Communications Career Supporting Entrepreneurship, Inclusion, and EU Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are passionate about communications, digital storytelling, European cooperation, entrepreneurship, and creating meaningful social impact, the Communications and Digital Engagement Officer position at UNITEE – New European Business Confederation offers an outstanding opportunity to begin or advance your career in the heart of Europe.

Based in Brussels, Belgium, this full-time role is designed for an early-career communications professional who enjoys transforming complex ideas into engaging digital content while supporting European projects focused on entrepreneurship, diversity, inclusion, vocational education, and social innovation.
