Successful candidates will work alongside an international team, contribute to European advocacy initiatives, support digital campaigns, organize events, and create compelling multimedia content that reaches stakeholders across Europe.

Application Deadline: Monday, 20 July 2026, 18:00 CET.

About UNITEE

UNITEE (New European Business Confederation) is a European network representing entrepreneurs and business professionals from diverse cultural backgrounds.

The organization collaborates with:

  • National business federations
  • Business support organizations
  • Civil society organizations
  • Academic institutions
  • Public authorities
  • European partners

UNITEE works to strengthen entrepreneurship ecosystems by promoting:

  • Inclusive entrepreneurship
  • Sustainable economic growth
  • Diversity, equity and inclusion
  • Innovation
  • European cooperation
  • Equal business opportunities

Through advocacy, communication campaigns, networking, coaching, and EU-funded projects, UNITEE helps entrepreneurs from underrepresented communities establish, grow, and expand successful businesses across Europe.

About the Communications and Digital Engagement Officer Position

UNITEE is recruiting a Communications and Digital Engagement Officer to join its Brussels office beginning in September 2026.

This position supports communication and dissemination activities across various European initiatives while helping strengthen UNITEE’s digital presence and organizational visibility.

The role is ideal for someone who enjoys:

  • Storytelling
  • Graphic design
  • Website management
  • Social media
  • Digital campaigns
  • Event communications
  • European affairs
  • Content creation

Applicants do not need to satisfy every listed requirement. UNITEE welcomes candidates with different experiences who demonstrate enthusiasm, creativity, willingness to learn, and a commitment to inclusion.

Key Responsibilities

The successful candidate will support communication activities across several areas.

1. Communications and Content Creation

Responsibilities include:

  • Planning communication campaigns
  • Supporting dissemination activities for advocacy and European projects
  • Creating engaging social media content
  • Writing articles
  • Developing newsletters
  • Producing digital campaigns
  • Designing presentations
  • Creating promotional materials
  • Producing success stories
  • Preparing communication reports
  • Monitoring campaign performance
  • Creating editorial calendars
  • Simplifying complex European policy topics into accessible content

2. Website and Digital Platform Management

The Communications Officer will also:

  • Update UNITEE project websites
  • Work with WordPress
  • Use Elementor
  • Publish:
    • News
    • Events
    • Publications
    • Project results
  • Improve website usability
  • Maintain consistent branding
  • Support online visibility campaigns
  • Develop digital resources

3. Design and Multimedia Production

A major component of the role focuses on visual communication.

The successful candidate will create:

  • Brochures
  • Infographics
  • Reports
  • Presentations
  • Social media graphics
  • Event materials
  • Promotional assets
  • Videos
  • Podcasts
  • Interviews

They will also:

  • Develop design templates
  • Maintain brand consistency
  • Follow emerging communication and design trends

4. Events and Stakeholder Engagement

The officer will support numerous European events, including:

  • Conferences
  • Workshops
  • Webinars
  • Networking events
  • Roundtables

Responsibilities include:

  • Preparing event materials
  • Developing participant information
  • Supporting event promotion
  • Managing communication before, during and after events
  • Creating visibility materials

Educational Requirements

Applicants should possess a degree in one of the following fields:

  • Communications
  • Marketing
  • Journalism
  • European Studies
  • Related disciplines

Professional Experience Required

Candidates should have:

  • At least one year of relevant experience

Experience may come from:

  • Professional employment
  • Internships
  • Volunteering
  • Community projects
  • Student organizations
  • NGO work

Experience with:

  • NGOs
  • European organizations
  • EU-funded projects

is considered an advantage.

Required Skills

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Excellent written English
  • Excellent spoken English
  • Strong visual communication skills
  • Creativity
  • Good organizational skills
  • Attention to detail
  • Social media communication experience
  • Content creation skills
  • Canva experience
  • Adobe Express experience (or similar tools)
  • Familiarity with newsletter platforms
  • Email marketing tools

Personal Qualities UNITEE Values

UNITEE is looking for candidates who are:

  • Curious
  • Creative
  • Proactive
  • Flexible
  • Respectful
  • Collaborative
  • Independent
  • Passionate about European cooperation
  • Interested in diversity and inclusion
  • Enthusiastic about entrepreneurship
  • Motivated to keep learning

Portfolio Encouraged

Applicants are encouraged to include examples of work such as:

  • Graphic design
  • Social media campaigns
  • Videos
  • Websites
  • Presentations
  • Marketing materials
  • Communication portfolios

Benefits of Working at UNITEE

Successful candidates will receive:

  • One-year fixed-term Belgian employment contract (CDD)
  • Possibility of contract renewal
  • Competitive salary appropriate for an early-career professional
  • Reimbursement of Brussels public transport (STIB/MIVB pass)
  • Exposure to European Union-funded projects
  • Opportunities to work on advocacy initiatives
  • Experience in European communications
  • Multicultural working environment
  • Professional growth opportunities
  • Networking with European stakeholders
  • Practical experience in communications, digital engagement, and policy dissemination

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

This role provides significantly more than a traditional communications position.

Employees will gain practical experience in:

  • European project communications
  • EU advocacy
  • Policy dissemination
  • Digital storytelling
  • Website management
  • Event communications
  • International stakeholder engagement
  • Multimedia production
  • Inclusive entrepreneurship initiatives

It offers an excellent pathway into European institutions, international NGOs, communications agencies, and public policy organizations.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit:

  1. An updated CV
  2. A short motivation letter

Applications should be emailed to:

info@unitee.eu

Use the subject line:

APPLICATION: Communication and Digital Engagement Officer

Applicants are encouraged to apply early, as UNITEE reviews applications on a rolling basis and may close recruitment before the advertised deadline if a suitable candidate is identified.

Application Deadline: Monday, 20 July 2026, 18:00 CET

Work Location

Brussels, Belgium

Expected start date:

September 2026

Why You Should Apply

If you are passionate about communications, digital engagement, European cooperation, entrepreneurship, and social impact, this position offers an excellent opportunity to build your career while contributing to projects that promote diversity, inclusion, innovation, and sustainable economic development across Europe. Working at UNITEE provides valuable international exposure, practical communications experience, and the chance to collaborate with experts, policymakers, entrepreneurs, and organizations from across the European Union.

For more information visit the official website

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Communications and Digital Engagement Officer at UNITEE (Brussels, Belgium): Apply for a European Communications Career Supporting Entrepreneurship, Inclusion, and EU Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Taylor’s University Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) in Business 2026: Complete Guide to Admission, Fees, Duration, Research Areas, Scholarships & Career Opportunities
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
Pursuing a Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) in Business is one of the highest academic achievements for professionals, researchers, educators, and future industry leaders seeking to contribute meaningful knowledge to the global business landscape. Taylor’s University, one of Malaysia’s leading private universities, offers a comprehensive PhD in Business programme that equips candidates with advanced research capabilities […]

The post Taylor’s University Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) in Business 2026: Complete Guide to Admission, Fees, Duration, Research Areas, Scholarships & Career Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Pursuing a Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) in Business is one of the highest academic achievements for professionals, researchers, educators, and future industry leaders seeking to contribute meaningful knowledge to the global business landscape. Taylor’s University, one of Malaysia’s leading private universities, offers a comprehensive PhD in Business programme that equips candidates with advanced research capabilities while allowing them to investigate real-world business challenges.

Whether your interests lie in entrepreneurship, finance, marketing, supply chain management, leadership, or human capital development, this doctoral programme provides an excellent platform to develop innovative solutions that influence academia, industry, and public policy.

Applications are currently open across multiple intakes in 2026, giving prospective candidates flexibility in planning their doctoral journey.

Why Study the PhD in Business at Taylor’s University?

Taylor’s University has established itself as one of Asia’s leading higher education institutions, known for academic excellence, impactful research, and strong industry collaboration.

The Doctor of Philosophy in Business is specifically designed for individuals who wish to:

  • Develop advanced research competencies.
  • Produce original contributions to business knowledge.
  • Address contemporary business and economic challenges.
  • Work closely with internationally recognised academics.
  • Build expertise that supports careers in academia, consulting, policymaking, entrepreneurship, and executive leadership.

Unlike coursework-based postgraduate degrees, this programme focuses extensively on independent research guided by experienced supervisors.

Programme Overview

The Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) in Business allows students to conduct rigorous research across numerous business disciplines while developing strong analytical, methodological, and critical thinking skills.

Students have the opportunity to investigate topics including:

  • Entrepreneurship
  • Human Capital Development
  • Financial Economics
  • Investment
  • Marketing
  • Supply Chain Management
  • Managerial Leadership
  • Business Innovation
  • Strategic Management
  • Organisational Behaviour
  • Corporate Governance
  • International Business
  • Business Sustainability

Throughout the programme, students are encouraged to generate original knowledge that contributes to academic literature while offering practical solutions to business challenges.

Because Taylor’s prioritises quality supervision, only a limited number of doctoral candidates are admitted during each intake, allowing supervisors to provide personalised academic guidance.

Research Environment

One of the strongest advantages of the programme is its research-focused learning environment.

Students benefit from:

  • Supervision by experienced academics
  • Access to internationally recognised researchers
  • Collaboration opportunities
  • Research seminars
  • Academic workshops
  • Professional networking
  • Publication support
  • Access to modern research facilities

Taylor’s also highlights that many of its academics are recognised among the Stanford World’s Top 2% Scientists, offering students valuable mentorship throughout their doctoral studies.

Programme Duration

The programme offers flexible study options suitable for both working professionals and full-time researchers.

Full-Time

  • 3 to 6 years

Part-Time

  • 4 to 8 years

This flexibility enables candidates to balance research with professional or personal commitments.

Study Modes

Applicants may choose from:

  • Full-Time
  • Part-Time

The programme also offers:

  • Normal Mode
  • Prior Publication Mode

This flexibility accommodates candidates with different research backgrounds and academic goals.

2026 Intake Months

Taylor’s University offers several admission opportunities throughout the year.

Available intakes include:

  • February
  • April
  • June
  • September
  • October

Since admissions are selective, prospective applicants are encouraged to apply early.

Tuition Fees

The published tuition fees for 2026 are:

Local Students

Normal Mode

  • MYR 51,560

Prior Publication Mode

  • MYR 34,640

International Students

Normal Mode

  • USD 18,474

Prior Publication Mode

  • USD 13,589

Applicants should note:

  • Fees are reviewed annually.
  • Effective 1 July 2025, a 6% Service Tax applies to non-Malaysian citizens on applicable fees (excluding International Security Deposit and EMGS application fees).

Scholarships and Financial Support

Taylor’s University encourages prospective doctoral students to explore available scholarship opportunities and financial aid schemes that may help reduce the cost of study.

Applicants are advised to review the university’s scholarship offerings before applying.

Why Choose Taylor’s University for Your PhD?

Several factors distinguish Taylor’s PhD in Business from many doctoral programmes.

These include:

1. Research Excellence

Students conduct original research addressing contemporary business challenges.

2. Expert Academic Supervision

Candidates receive guidance from internationally recognised scholars.

3. Flexible Learning

Both full-time and part-time options are available.

4. Multiple Annual Intakes

Five admission periods provide flexibility for applicants.

5. Strong Research Community

Students collaborate with fellow researchers and industry experts.

6. Career Development

Graduates gain advanced research, analytical, leadership, and academic skills.

Admission Process

Taylor’s University has streamlined the application process for prospective doctoral candidates.

Applicants should:

  1. Review admission requirements.
  2. Prepare all required academic documents.
  3. Submit an online application.
  4. Provide any additional documentation requested.
  5. Await confirmation from the admissions office.

The university provides comprehensive admissions guidance through its official admissions portal.

Who Should Apply?

This programme is ideal for:

  • University lecturers
  • Academic researchers
  • Business professionals
  • Consultants
  • Policy analysts
  • Entrepreneurs
  • Corporate executives
  • Government officers
  • NGO professionals
  • Individuals seeking careers in research or higher education

Candidates with strong research interests and a passion for advancing business knowledge will particularly benefit from the programme.

Career Opportunities

Graduates of the Doctor of Philosophy in Business can pursue careers across academia, government, multinational corporations, consulting firms, research institutions, and international organisations.

Potential career pathways include:

  • University Professor
  • Lecturer
  • Senior Research Fellow
  • Business Consultant
  • Corporate Strategist
  • Policy Researcher
  • Economist
  • Executive Director
  • Innovation Manager
  • Entrepreneurship Advisor
  • Business Development Director
  • Research and Development Manager
  • Think Tank Researcher

The advanced research skills acquired throughout the programme are highly valued across numerous industries.

Academic Experience at Taylor’s University

Taylor’s University emphasises an ecosystem that nurtures intellectual development, creativity, and practical wisdom.

Students benefit from:

  • Collaborative learning
  • Industry engagement
  • Alumni networking
  • Academic conferences
  • Research seminars
  • Cross-disciplinary collaboration
  • Modern campus facilities

This environment supports both academic excellence and professional growth.

Important Information

Applicants should note:

  • Limited doctoral places are available each intake.
  • Early application is highly recommended.
  • Tuition fees are subject to annual review.
  • International students may be subject to additional government-related charges and taxes.

Learn more and Apply Here

Final Thoughts

Taylor’s University continues to position itself as a leading destination for postgraduate research in Asia. Its Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) in Business programme offers aspiring researchers the opportunity to investigate critical business issues, contribute original scholarship, and work alongside internationally recognised academics.

With flexible study options, multiple annual intakes, comprehensive research support, and access to world-class supervision, this doctoral programme provides an excellent pathway for individuals seeking careers in academia, research, policy, or executive leadership. Prospective candidates interested in shaping the future of business through rigorous research should consider applying early to secure a place in this competitive programme.

For more information, visit the official website

Discover global scholarship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Taylor’s University Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) in Business 2026: Complete Guide to Admission, Fees, Duration, Research Areas, Scholarships & Career Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
